summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing to continue deliberation on text amendment Case ZC 2021-001, which proposes changes to the zoning resolution regarding lot coverage percentages, accessory structure square footage limits, and maximum height of accessory buildings. The commission voted to recommend a tiered lot coverage system (30% for lots under half acre, 40% for half acre to one acre, 50% for lots over one acre), deletion of the 2,000 sq ft accessory structure cap, no maximum square footage for accessory buildings in any zoning district, and an 18-foot maximum height for accessory structures. These recommendations will go to the Board of Trustees for final approval.
decisions: Approved meeting minutes from February 2nd meeting
Approved proposed 7.01 lot coverage: 30% for lots under half acre, 40% for half acre to one acre, 50% for lots over one acre including principal building and accessory structures
Approved deletion of existing 7.01C2B (2,000 sq ft aggregate cap on accessory structures)
Approved proposed 7.01C2C: no maximum square footage of accessory buildings or structures in any zoning district
Approved proposed 7.01C3: maximum height of accessory structures shall be 18 feet, measured per Section 5.05A4
action_items: Staff to forward zoning commission recommendations on text amendment ZC 2021-001 to Board of Trustees for final approval
Fill one remaining alternate position on Zoning Commission
key_amounts: 30% maximum lot coverage for lots under half acre (Zoning Commission recommendation)
40% maximum lot coverage for lots half acre to one acre (Zoning Commission recommendation)
50% maximum lot coverage for lots over one acre (Zoning Commission recommendation vs Trustees' 50% across the board)
2,000 square feet accessory structure cap deleted
18 feet maximum height for accessory structures (reduced from existing 25 feet)
6,000 square feet typical lot size in Woodland Streams subdivision discussed
1,400 square feet minimum house size referenced
notable_topics: Text amendment ZC 2021-001 to Goshen Township zoning resolution
Lot coverage percentage changes for residential districts
Removal of 2,000 sq ft aggregate cap on accessory structures
Accessory structure maximum height reduction from 25 feet to 18 feet
Clermont County Planning Commission recommendation of approval
Impact on small subdivision lots and concerns about lot coverage on quarter-acre lots
Agricultural use exemptions for accessory building height
notes: This was a special meeting since the Zoning Commission usually meets at the beginning of the month. The commission's recommendations differ from the Board of Trustees' original proposal, particularly on lot coverage (tiered percentages vs flat 50%) and height (18 feet vs 25 feet). The Clermont County Planning Commission voted to recommend approval of the Trustees' version. The Board of Trustees has final approval authority. COVID-19 social distancing protocols were noted as still in effect. Next meeting scheduled for May 4, 2021 at 6:30 PM. One alternate position remains open on the Zoning Commission. Audio quality appears poor with numerous transcript artifacts.
title: ZC 3-23-21
